# Antigravity Platform Context & Guidelines

## Overview
Antigravity is a high-performance, privacy-first online document conversion platform built on Next.js/React and WebAssembly (Wasm). All document processing must happen securely on the user's local machine inside the browser.

## Core Pillars
When working on this repository, you must adhere strictly to the following four pillars:

### Pillar 1: Core Technical Architecture (The Engine)
* **100% Client-Side Processing:** All document conversions (DOCX to PDF, merge, split, compress) must execute locally inside the user's browser via a WebAssembly (Wasm) engine. Files must NEVER be transmitted to, read by, or stored on a remote backend server.
* **Offline Capability & Speed:** The engine registers web workers efficiently so processing works even offline once the assets are loaded. Optimize bundle sizes to keep TTFB and LCP within Google Core Web Vitals thresholds.
* **Unlimited File Size Handling:** Because processing utilizes the user's local CPU and RAM, there are no server-side file size restrictions.

### Pillar 2: Technical SEO & Schema Automation
* **Dynamic Metadata Generator:** Every tool route dynamically generates unique `<title>` and `<meta name="description">` tags targeting long-tail, high-intent privacy keywords (e.g., "Convert DOCX to PDF Offline," "100% Private Word to PDF Converter").
* **Structured Data (JSON-LD):** Automatically inject valid JSON-LD schema into the `<head>` of tool pages, including `SoftwareApplication` (category UtilitiesApplication, price 0.00) and `FAQPage` schemas for rich snippets.

### Pillar 3: UX/UI & Conversion Optimization
* **Above-The-Fold Trust Signals:** Visually prominent badges are rendered immediately adjacent to the primary dropzone:
  * 🔒 100% Local & Private (Files never leave your browser)
  * ⚡ Instant WebAssembly Processing
  * 📂 Unlimited File Size & Free Forever
* **Ad-Placement Hygiene:** Ad container slots are structured strictly below the primary interactive conversion tool or confined to non-blocking side rails to prevent Top Heavy algorithm penalties.
* **State Feedback:** Provide clear, multi-stage visual progress indicators during processing (e.g., Reading local file... -> Compiling layout via Wasm... -> Ready for Download).

### Pillar 4: Content Strategy & Keyword Targeting
* **Benefit-Driven H1s:** Use action-oriented, privacy-focused H1s such as "Convert Word to PDF Securely Inside Your Browser — No Server Uploads Required."
* **Target Long-Tail Privacy Keywords:** Attract users searching for secure alternatives (HIPAA compliant PDF tool, confidential document converter).
* **Semantic Hierarchy:** Structure all text using clean H1 -> H2 -> H3 markdown/HTML tags.

## Execution Instructions for AI
1. Always output complete, production-ready code or text.
2. Ensure accessibility standards (WCAG AA) are met with proper ARIA labels, semantic roles, and keyboard navigation.
3. Explicitly comment in the code where SEO schemas, Wasm hooks, and trust badges are integrated.
